description: ID from author list of those who assembled the genome(s)
Publication-ready or template-generated text describing the assembly method.
Software/Pipelines used for assembly generation, scaffolding or purging, for example Hifiasm, purge_dups or YaHS. Should be listed in the references.
Whether the contig assembly was generated in a haplotype-aware mode using Hi-C, parental or other data. NB: HiFiasm is not the only assembler

Command-line options passed to the contig assemblier where these affect the published assembly method, for example --primary -l0. NB: Setting -l0 disables internal purging, so this is reduntant with the option below
Publication-ready sentence derived from Hifiasm options by data_note.

Whether the contig assembler generated primary/alternate contig sets, or haplotype sets. NB: I also feel this is reduntant with the CLI options above, but maybe good to keep in case other assemblers have different modes
Organelle assembly steps or decisions, including assembler and comparator where relevant.
Tool or workflow used for organelle assembly, for example MitoHiFi or Oatk. Should be a link to an object in the reference list
Comparator species, assembly or reference used for organelle assembly, where applicable.
Short note on organelle assembly choices or review decisions.
This should be Primary, Alternate OR Primary followed by Haplotype_{int}. This should act as a key where noted HAP_KEY
